$comment: Vibe-Appliance metadata for Vibe-Connect. Read by the appliance bootstrap to render compose, env templates, and the admin console panel. Supports both path-prefix mode (BASE_PATH=/connect) and multi-subdomain mode (connect.<firm>.com staff + client.<firm>.com portal). Per-app subdomain entries live in the appliance repo's console/manifests/vibe-connect.json subdomains[] array.
description: End-to-end encrypted messaging and document sharing for CPA firms and their clients

audiences.name: staff
audiences.note: Staff SPA + /api proxy. Caddy upstream terminates TLS in appliance mode (TLS_MODE=external).

audiences.name: portal
audiences.note: Client portal SPA + /portal proxy. Same nginx image, different port.

comment_no_redis: Realtime fanout uses Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY (apps/server/src/realtime/pgFanout.ts). Redis is NOT required.

required.name: BACKUP_HEARTBEAT_TOKEN
required.doc: Bearer token Duplicati POSTs to /admin/backup-heartbeat. Required when BACKUP_REQUIRED=true.

optional.name: EMAIL_PROVIDER
optional.doc: mock | postmark | postfix | none. Set to 'none' to ship without outbound mail; portal access codes still work via SMS.

optional.name: SMS_PROVIDER
optional.doc: mock | textlink | twilio. Default 'mock' so a misconfigured appliance doesn't accidentally bill the firm.

firstLogin.note: First POST /install creates the firm key and returns the 24-word recovery phrase. The phrase is shown ONCE and never stored server-side. The operator MUST capture it; loss of both the recovery phrase and a Postgres backup is unrecoverable. Send the install URL to the firm partner who will manage user provisioning.

migrations.note: Set MIGRATIONS_AUTO=false in the appliance overlay so the bootstrap runs migrations centrally instead of each container racing on startup.

backup.tlsNote: The /var/lib/vibe/connect/tls bind-mount is intentionally NOT in criticalVolumes. ACME-issued certs are re-derivable; backing them up is fine but losing them just means a re-issue, not data loss. Critical state is the postgres-data volume (firm key + all conversation/vault metadata) and the uploads volume (attachment + vault file ciphertext).

desktopDistribution.note: The /desktop/ redirect is mounted only on the staff port (443), NOT the portal port (8443). Clients have no use for the desktop wrapper.

outOfScopeForV1: Code-signed Windows binaries — Phase B v1 ships unsigned MSI/NSIS; SmartScreen warning documented in docs/ops/DESKTOP.md.
Magic-link client auth — repo uses access codes (email or SMS); not changing.
Redis / BullMQ — not in the codebase; realtime uses Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY.
